title: lophiolepis spathulata
intro: Lophiolepis spathulata, also known as the Spatulate Club Moss, is a species of plant that belongs to the genus Lophiolepis. It is a type of ancient, non-vascular plant that is native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia. The Spatulate Club Moss is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is used to treat various health conditions, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Lophiolepis spathulata is often made into teas, infusions, or decoctions, and is also used in traditional medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
